title: pleopeltis alborufula
intro: Pleopeltis alborufula, also known as the Alborough Polypody, is a species of fern found in tropical regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Pleopeltis alborufula include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and it is also used as a component in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
